mission.title: kepler: VM: all node / pod energy report 0 (again..)
mission.description: VM: all node / pod energy report 0 (again..). Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kepler deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kepler version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
helm list -n kepler 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kepler installation.

steps.title: Review kepler configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kepler configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
kubectl get configmap -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kepler
```
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

after use latest update with a few enhancement, my pod/node report energy become 0 again.. 
switch to v0.3 I can see the data correctly reported ,...

steps.title: Apply the fix for VM: all node / pod energy report 0 (again..)
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
kubectl get events -n kepler --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "VM: all node / pod energy report 0 (again..)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/sustainable-computing-io/kepler/pull/377

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kepler installed or the issue environment reproducible.
